name: llvm.abs
description: Integer absolute value with defined overflow behavior; used in numeric code, distance/magnitude calculations, and branchless abs on vectors.

name: llvm.acos
description: Floating-point arccosine; used in geometry, signal processing, and graphics shading.

name: llvm.addressofreturnaddress
description: Returns the address where the current function’s return address is stored; used for low-level stack introspection, sanitizers, and security instrumentation.

name: llvm.adjust.trampoline
description: Adjusts a trampoline pointer to a callable target per ABI; used to implement nested functions/closures that need a static chain.

name: llvm.allow.runtime.check
description: Hint that introducing runtime checks is semantically allowed; used to enable loop versioning, alias/bounds checks, and speculative optimizations.

name: llvm.allow.ubsan.check
description: Hint to preserve/allow UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer checks on a value; used to prevent optimizations from eliding sanitizer diagnostics.

name: llvm.annotation
description: Attaches a frontend annotation string to a value (returns the value); used by __attribute__((annotate)) for tooling, analysis, and optimization guidance.

name: llvm.arithmetic.fence
description: Prevents reassociation and certain arithmetic transformations across it; used to enforce evaluation order (e.g., FP exceptions) or to isolate regions for analysis.

name: llvm.asan.check.memaccess
description: AddressSanitizer memory access check for a load/store; inserted to validate accesses at runtime and report out-of-bounds/use-after-free.

name: llvm.asin
description: Floating-point arcsine; used in geometry, signal processing, and graphics shading.

name: llvm.assume
description: Asserts a condition to the optimizer (undefined if false) without generating code; used for range info, vectorization, and dead-code elimination guidance.

name: llvm.atan
description: Floating-point arctangent; used in angle computations, coordinate transforms, and signal processing.

name: llvm.bitreverse
description: Reverses the bit order of an integer; used in FFT bit-reversal permutations, cryptography, and bitboard algorithms.

name: llvm.bswap
description: Reverses the byte order of an integer; used for endianness conversions (e.g., network/host order) and fast serialization.

name: llvm.call.preallocated.arg
description: Associates a caller-provided preallocated buffer with a call’s preallocated return/result; used to pass storage to callees and avoid dynamic allocation.

name: llvm.call.preallocated.setup
description: Sets up (and returns) the pointer/token for a call’s preallocated return storage before the call; used to prepare caller-side buffers.

name: llvm.call.preallocated.teardown
description: Tears down the preallocated return storage after the call (lifetime end); used to finalize caller-provided result buffers.

name: llvm.callbr.landingpad
description: Produces an EH landing-pad-like token for callbr/asm-goto unwinding; used to model exceptional control flow with callbr/inline asm.

name: llvm.canonicalize
description: Canonicalizes a floating value (e.g., -0 to +0, sNaN to qNaN); used to normalize results for reproducibility and simplify comparisons/hashing.

name: llvm.ceil
description: Floating-point ceiling (round toward +∞); used in tiling, grid/texture mapping, and size/alignment computations.

name: llvm.clear_cache
description: Flushes the instruction cache for an address range; used by JITs, self-modifying code, and dynamic code loaders to ensure newly generated code is executable.

name: llvm.codeview.annotation
description: Emits a CodeView debug annotation string (no runtime effect); used to support MSVC __annotation and tooling markers in PDBs for diagnostics/profiling.

name: llvm.convert.from.fp16
description: Converts an IEEE-754 binary16 bit pattern (i16) to a wider float/double; used on targets without native half to read half-precision data.

name: llvm.convert.to.fp16
description: Converts a float/double to an IEEE-754 binary16 bit pattern (i16) with rounding; used to store/pack half-precision values on targets without native half.

name: llvm.copysign
description: Returns the first operand with its sign bit set to the second operand’s sign; used to implement copysign in numeric code and sign manipulation.

name: llvm.coro.align
description: Yields the required alignment for the coroutine frame; used to allocate the frame with correct alignment (e.g., operator new alignment).

name: llvm.coro.alloc
description: Indicates whether the coroutine frame needs dynamic allocation; used to guard/skip calling the frame allocator when elidable.

name: llvm.coro.alloca.alloc
description: Reserves dynamic stack-like storage that may be promoted into the coroutine frame across suspends; used for variable-size allocas in coroutines.

name: llvm.coro.alloca.free
description: Marks the end of the lifetime of storage from coro.alloca.alloc; used to free such storage at the right point across coroutine splits.

name: llvm.coro.alloca.get
description: Produces the usable pointer for storage reserved by coro.alloca.alloc given the coroutine frame; used to access that memory after setup.

name: llvm.coro.async.context.alloc
description: Allocates the async coroutine context/state block; used by async coroutine lowering to create state for resumption.

name: llvm.coro.async.context.dealloc
description: Frees the async coroutine context/state block; used when an async coroutine completes or is destroyed.

name: llvm.coro.async.resume
description: Resumes an async coroutine using its context; used to implement resumption paths for co_await/co_return.

name: llvm.coro.async.size.replace
description: Replaces a placeholder size for the async context with a known constant post-splitting; used to fix allocation size after coroutine analysis.

name: llvm.coro.await.suspend.bool
description: Lowers await_suspend returning bool: sets up suspension and yields the decision to suspend; used for awaiters that may choose not to suspend.

name: llvm.coro.await.suspend.handle
description: Lowers await_suspend returning coroutine_handle: sets up suspension and obtains the continuation handle; used for awaiters that redirect continuation.

name: llvm.coro.await.suspend.void
description: Lowers await_suspend returning void: sets up an unconditional suspension; used for awaiters that always suspend.

name: llvm.coro.begin
description: Creates/initializes the coroutine frame and returns the coroutine handle; used at coroutine entry to set up state and use any allocated storage.

name: llvm.coro.destroy
description: Destroys a coroutine: runs final cleanup and releases its frame; used by co_destroy or when a coroutine is explicitly/implicitly destroyed.

name: llvm.coro.done
description: Tests whether the coroutine has reached its final suspend (completed); used to implement coroutine_handle::done() and completion checks.

name: llvm.coro.end
description: Marks coroutine termination and performs final-suspend/cleanup; used at co_return/final suspend to destroy/deallocate the frame.

name: llvm.coro.end.async
description: Async-specific end; signals completion and schedules continuation; used in async/await lowering for platform async runtimes.

name: llvm.coro.end.results
description: Extracts/propagates results produced at coroutine completion; used to materialize return values in async/retcon models.

name: llvm.coro.frame
description: Returns a pointer to the coroutine frame; used to access the promise and spilled state inside the coroutine.

name: llvm.coro.free
description: Deallocates a coroutine frame; used when destroying a coroutine or after completion/cancellation.

name: llvm.coro.id
description: Creates a coroutine identifier token; used to drive lowering, frame layout, and sub-function address queries.

name: llvm.coro.id.async
description: Creates an identifier for async coroutines; used by async/await lowering to build platform-specific state machines.

name: llvm.coro.id.retcon
description: Identifier for retcon (return-continuation) coroutines; used when suspension returns a continuation to the caller.

name: llvm.coro.id.retcon.once
description: Retcon ID for coroutines that suspend at most once; enables tighter frames and optimized lowering for single-suspend generators.

name: llvm.coro.noop
description: Produces a handle to a no-op coroutine (resume/destroy do nothing); useful for awaiters that never suspend or default continuations.

name: llvm.coro.prepare.async
description: Prepares an async coroutine for suspension/resumption with a continuation/context; used to hook callbacks in async runtimes.

name: llvm.coro.prepare.retcon
description: Prepares a retcon coroutine and constructs a returnable continuation object; used to hand a callable continuation back at suspend.

name: llvm.coro.promise
description: Returns the promise object address in the frame; used to exchange results/exceptions between the coroutine and its consumer.

name: llvm.coro.resume
description: Calls the coroutine’s resume sub-function; used by handles/runtimes to continue execution from a saved suspend point.

name: llvm.coro.save
description: Saves the current suspend point into the frame; used before co_await/co_yield to capture where to resume.

name: llvm.coro.size
description: Returns the required frame size in bytes; used to allocate storage for the coroutine frame (heap/arena/stack).

name: llvm.coro.subfn.addr
description: Returns address of a coroutine sub-function (resume/destroy/cleanup); used to build handles/vtables and wire resumption/destruction.

name: llvm.coro.suspend
description: Performs a suspend and yields control; used to implement co_await/co_yield/final-suspend and drive resume/cleanup paths.

name: llvm.coro.suspend.async
description: Async suspend integrating with platform continuations; used to schedule and return control in async/await lowering.

name: llvm.coro.suspend.retcon
description: Retcon suspend that packages a continuation to be returned; used when suspension returns a callable continuation to the caller.

name: llvm.eh.sjlj.lsda
description: Returns the function’s LSDA pointer for SJLJ exception handling; used in the dispatch block to select the right landing pad.

name: llvm.eh.sjlj.setjmp
description: Sets a jmp buffer for SJLJ EH; returns 0 initially and nonzero on unwind; used to establish the per-function exception dispatch point.

name: llvm.eh.sjlj.setup.dispatch
description: Sets up and registers the function’s SJLJ dispatch with the runtime; used at entry to prepare exception dispatch and landing pads.

name: llvm.eh.typeid.for
description: Computes a small integer type ID for a typeinfo pointer; used in C++ EH to test catch clauses and type filters.

name: llvm.eh.unwind.init
description: Initializes or marks the start of unwindable state for the function; used by EH lowering on targets that require explicit prologue marking.

name: llvm.exp
description: Natural exponential e^x; used in scientific kernels, normalization, and numeric transforms.

name: llvm.exp10
description: Base-10 exponential 10^x; used in decimal scaling and inverse log10 computations.

name: llvm.exp2
description: Base-2 exponential 2^x; used in exponent scaling, bit-level transforms, and DSP.

name: llvm.expect
description: Branch prediction hint that a value is likely equal to an expected value; guides code layout and static prediction.

name: llvm.expect.with.probability
description: Prediction hint with an explicit probability; attaches precise branch weights from heuristics or PGO.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.acos
description: Strict IEEE acos that respects FP environment and exceptions; used under strict or fenv-sensitive FP modes.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.asin
description: Strict IEEE asin that respects FP environment and exceptions; used when preserving fenv and reproducibility.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.atan
description: Strict IEEE atan that respects FP environment and exceptions; used for strict or precise FP semantics.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.ceil
description: Strict ceil with preserved FP exception behavior and no unsafe reordering; used under strict FP models.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.cos
description: Strict IEEE cos that respects FP environment and exceptions; used in fenv-aware or reproducible math code.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.cosh
description: Strict IEEE cosh that respects FP environment and exceptions; used when fenv and exceptions must be honored.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.exp
description: Strict IEEE exp that respects rounding mode and FP exceptions; used under strict FP or fenv-sensitive code.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.exp2
description: Strict IEEE exp2 that respects rounding mode and FP exceptions; used for precise FP semantics.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.fadd
description: Strict floating add honoring specified rounding and exception behavior; used to preserve IEEE semantics and block reassociation.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.fcmp
description: Strict floating compare preserving IEEE NaN and exception semantics; used when comparisons may raise exceptions and must not be reordered.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.fcmps
description: Signaling FP compare with strict IEEE semantics (may raise on NaNs); used when comparisons must honor FP exceptions/flags in strict/precise FP modes.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.fdiv
description: Strict floating-point division respecting rounding mode and exception behavior; used for reproducible/exception-aware division in strict FP code.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.floor
description: Strict floor (round toward −∞) with correct FP flag behavior; used where IEEE exceptions/flags must be preserved.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.fma
description: Strict fused multiply-add (one rounding) honoring FP env; used for reproducible FMAs in numerics and performance-critical kernels.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.fmul
description: Strict floating-point multiply respecting rounding mode and exceptions; used when exact IEEE behavior is required.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.fmuladd
description: Strict multiply-add that may contract to FMA per constraints; used to express x*y+z with optional fusion while preserving FP env semantics.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.fpext
description: Strict floating-point widen (e.g., float→double) with correct FP flag tracking; used when conversions must honor the FP environment.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.fptosi
description: Strict FP-to-signed-int convert honoring rounding/exceptions; used when casts must trap/set flags per IEEE/C semantics.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.fptoui
description: Strict FP-to-unsigned-int convert honoring rounding/exceptions; used for precise casts that must respect FP env.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.fptrunc
description: Strict floating-point narrow (e.g., double→float) with specified rounding and exceptions; used for reproducible casts.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.frem
description: Strict FP remainder (like C fmod: x − trunc(x/y)*y) with correct FP flags; used when remainder must follow IEEE/C semantics.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.fsub
description: Strict floating-point subtraction respecting rounding mode and exceptions; used in strict/precise FP paths.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.ldexp
description: Strict ldexp: scale x by 2^exp with FP env semantics; used in constructing/scaling FP values and math libraries.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.llrint
description: Strict llrint: round to long long using current rounding mode, setting FP flags; used when honoring the thread’s rounding mode.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.llround
description: Strict llround: round to nearest, ties away from zero, to long long with proper exceptions; used for deterministic integer rounding.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.log
description: Strict natural logarithm with FP env semantics; used in libm and code needing reproducible exceptions/rounding.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.log10
description: Strict base-10 logarithm with FP env semantics; used where decimal-scale logs must honor exceptions/rounding.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.log2
description: Strict base-2 logarithm with FP env semantics; used in algorithms needing log2 with precise IEEE behavior.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.lrint
description: Strict lrint: round to long using current rounding mode, setting FP flags; used when the active rounding mode must be obeyed.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.lround
description: Strict lround: round to nearest, ties away from zero, to long with proper exceptions; used for stable, standards-compliant rounding.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.maximum
description: IEEE-754-2019 maximum (total order; propagates NaN); constrained FP for strict max with correct NaN/-0/+0 handling.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.maxnum
description: IEEE-754 maxNum (like C fmax): numeric maximum, ignore single NaN; constrained FP for strict min/max and reductions.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.minimum
description: IEEE-754-2019 minimum (total order; propagates NaN); constrained FP for strict min with correct NaN/-0/+0 handling.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.minnum
description: IEEE-754 minNum (like C fmin): numeric minimum, ignore single NaN; constrained FP for strict min and reductions.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.nearbyint
description: Round to integral using current rounding mode, no inexact; constrained FP for fenv-aware rounding.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.pow
description: x^y with IEEE exceptions/rounding control; constrained FP for strict libm pow in scientific code.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.powi
description: x^n with integer exponent; constrained FP, optimizable by squaring; tight loops/polynomials.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.rint
description: Round to integral using current rounding mode, may raise inexact; constrained FP for fenv-aware rounding.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.round
description: Round to nearest, ties away from zero; constrained FP for strict C round.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.roundeven
description: Round to nearest, ties-to-even; constrained FP for banker’s rounding in finance/signal processing.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.sin
description: Sine with controlled exceptions/rounding; constrained FP for strict libm sin in DSP/graphics.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.sinh
description: Hyperbolic sine with controlled exceptions/rounding; constrained FP for scientific computing.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.sitofp
description: Signed int to float with specified rounding/exception behavior; constrained FP for trapping or fenv-aware conversions.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.sqrt
description: Square root with controlled exceptions/rounding; constrained FP for precise sqrt in numerics/graphics.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.tan
description: Tangent with controlled exceptions/rounding; constrained FP for strict trig math.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.tanh
description: Hyperbolic tangent with controlled exceptions/rounding; constrained FP for ML activations/numerics.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.trunc
description: Round toward zero; constrained FP for strict C trunc regardless of current mode.

name: llvm.experimental.constrained.uitofp
description: Unsigned int to float with specified rounding/exception behavior; constrained FP for strict conversions.

name: llvm.experimental.convergence.anchor
description: Marks a dynamic convergence point; prevents code motion/duplication across it; used for GPU/SIMT convergent ops (ballot, barrier).

name: llvm.experimental.convergence.entry
description: Marks start/root of a convergence region for threads; used at GPU kernel/parallel entry to preserve reconvergence.

name: llvm.experimental.vp.splice
description: Predicated vector splice: combine two vectors by shifting in elements from the second at a given offset under mask/EVL; used for sliding-window shifts and cross-lane moves (e.g., SVE/RVV) in stencil/FIR kernels.

name: llvm.experimental.vp.strided.load
description: Predicated strided vector load with explicit vector length; loads base + i*stride for active lanes; used for vectorizing affine non-unit-stride accesses (AoS/SoA, column loads).

name: llvm.experimental.vp.strided.store
description: Predicated strided vector store with explicit vector length; stores to base + i*stride for active lanes; used for vectorized non-unit-stride stores and interleaved data layouts.

name: llvm.experimental.widenable.condition
description: A condition the optimizer may only widen to true; AND it with guards to enable unswitching/versioning/speculation while preserving correctness; used in guards and loop optimizations.

name: llvm.fabs
description: Floating-point absolute value; used in numeric algorithms, convergence/error checks, distance computations.

name: llvm.floor
description: Round toward −∞ (largest integer ≤ x); used in indexing, binning, discretization, and math libs.

name: llvm.fma
description: Fused multiply-add with a single rounding; used in dot products, polynomial evaluation, and BLAS kernels.

name: llvm.fmuladd
description: Compute x*y+z with permission to fuse (legacy hint for FMA/contraction); used to expose FMA opportunities under fast-math; prefer fma or FMF/contract today.

name: llvm.fptosi.sat
description: Float-to-signed-int conversion with saturation (clamps out-of-range; NaN→0); used in image/DSP and safe fixed-point conversions without UB.

name: llvm.fptoui.sat
description: Float-to-unsigned-int conversion with saturation (clamps to [0,max]; NaN→0); used in image/DSP and robust parsing/fixed-point conversion.

name: llvm.fptrunc.round
description: Narrow FP value with an explicit IEEE-754 rounding mode; used for reproducible rounding, language-defined rounding, and vectorized FP conversions.

name: llvm.frameaddress
description: Return address of the current/caller’s stack frame (by level); used for stack walking, debugging, low-level runtime tricks; target/ABI-dependent.

name: llvm.frexp
description: Decompose x into significand and exponent (x = mantissa*2^exp); used in math libraries for range reduction, log/pow, and classification.

name: llvm.fshl
description: Funnel shift left across two integers (concatenate then left shift/rotate); used in rotates, crypto, hashing, and bit twiddling.

name: llvm.fshr
description: Funnel shift right across two integers; used in rotates, crypto, hashing, and bit twiddling.

name: llvm.gcread
description: GC read barrier: model a read of a GC-managed reference with the collector’s required barrier semantics; used by GC-enabled frontends for precise/moving GC.

name: llvm.gcroot
description: Mark a stack slot as a GC root (shadow stack); used by frontends to make stack references visible to precise collectors.

name: llvm.gcwrite
description: GC write barrier: model writing a GC-managed reference with required barrier semantics; lowers to card marking/remembered-set updates.

name: llvm.get.active.lane.mask
description: Create a vector mask marking lanes in a given index range (start..end); used to materialize active-lane masks for scalable vector tails/predication (SVE/RVV).

name: llvm.get.dynamic.area.offset
description: Query the target-specific offset to the function’s dynamically sized stack area; used to form addresses into dynamic alloca/outgoing-call areas when offsets aren’t known until codegen.

name: llvm.is.fpclass
description: Tests whether a floating-point value is in any of the requested IEEE-754 classes (NaN, ±Inf, normal, subnormal, ±0); used to implement isnan/isinf/isfinite/issubnormal and fast FP-class checks via target instructions.

name: llvm.launder.invariant.group
description: Returns the same address with a fresh invariant-group identity; models object reinitialization (e.g., C++ placement new) so prior invariant loads (like vptr) aren’t reused; helps correct devirtualization and AA.

name: llvm.ldexp
description: Computes x * 2^exp with exact power-of-two scaling (vectorizable); used for efficient scaling, implementing ldexp/scalbn, and reconstructing values from mantissa/exponent.

name: llvm.lifetime.end
description: Marks the end of a stack object’s lifetime; enables early stack-slot reuse, dead-store elimination, and better register allocation; useful for alloca’d temporaries and SROA.

name: llvm.lifetime.start
description: Marks the beginning of a stack object’s lifetime; guides stack coloring and SROA, and can inhibit loads/stores before the object becomes live.

name: llvm.llrint
description: Rounds to nearest integer under current FP rounding mode and returns long long; may raise inexact/overflow per libm; for strict IEEE/C semantics when converting FP to integer.

name: llvm.llround
description: Rounds halfway away from zero to long long; no inexact flag, may overflow; used for C llround semantics in FP→integer conversions with specified tie-breaking.

name: llvm.load.relative
description: Loads via a relative reference: reads a signed addend from a slot, adds it to the slot’s address to form an absolute pointer, and loads the target; used for relative vtables/PIC without GOT (e.g., -frelative-vtables).

name: llvm.localaddress
description: Produces an opaque pointer identifying the current function for frame/local recovery; passed to outlined helpers and used with llvm.localrecover (MSVC/WinEH, outlined cleanups/finally).

name: llvm.localescape
description: Marks which allocas’ addresses must be recoverable outside the function; used by EH/outlining to export locals for access in helper functions; pairs with llvm.localrecover.

name: llvm.localrecover
description: In an outlined helper, recovers the address of an escaped alloca from the parent given the parent function id and frame pointer plus index; used in MSVC/WinEH and similar outlining schemes.

name: llvm.log
description: Natural logarithm; used in scientific/numerical code, normalization, and transforms; lowered to libm or hardware where available and honors fast-math flags.

name: llvm.log10
description: Base-10 logarithm; used in decibel calculations, human-readable scaling, and scientific formatting; maps to libm/hardware when possible.

name: llvm.log2
description: Base-2 logarithm; used for exponent extraction, scaling in power-of-two domains, and bit-level algorithms; maps to libm/hardware instructions where available.

name: llvm.loop.decrement
description: Decrement-and-test for counted loops; used by front ends/passes to form low-overhead hardware loops and lower to “decrement-and-branch-if-not-zero” sequences on supporting targets.

name: llvm.loop.decrement.reg
description: Register-based variant of the loop decrement-and-test; facilitates lowering to targets with dedicated loop counters or predicated decrement instructions for minimal loop overhead.

name: llvm.lrint
description: Rounds to nearest integer under current FP rounding mode and returns long; may raise inexact/overflow per libm; for strict IEEE/C lrint semantics.

name: llvm.lround
description: Rounds halfway away from zero to long; no inexact flag, may overflow; used for C lround semantics in FP→integer conversions.

name: llvm.masked.compressstore
description: Stores only lanes where the mask is true, packed contiguously in memory (compacts active elements); used in vectorized filtering/packing, lowers to AVX-512/SVE compress-store.

name: llvm.masked.expandload
description: Loads elements from memory and places them into lanes selected by a mask, expanding into sparse positions (inverse of compress); used in predicated vector loops, lowers to AVX-512/SVE expand-load.

name: llvm.masked.gather
description: Loads selected elements from computed addresses under a mask (others pass through); used in vectorizing irregular indirections (sparse lookups, table reads) and safe tail handling.

name: llvm.masked.load
description: Loads a vector with per-lane masking (inactive lanes pass through a value); used for predicated/tail loads in SIMD loops.

name: llvm.masked.scatter
description: Stores selected vector elements to computed addresses under a mask; used in vectorizing indirect writes and sparse updates.

name: llvm.masked.store
description: Stores a vector with per-lane masking; used for predicated/tail stores in SIMD loops.

name: llvm.matrix.column.major.load
description: Loads an M×N matrix from column-major memory with a stride into a matrix value; used to feed LLVM matrix ops and target HW matrix units.

name: llvm.matrix.column.major.store
description: Stores an M×N matrix value to memory in column-major layout with a stride; used after matrix ops for efficient layout handling.

name: llvm.matrix.multiply
description: Matrix multiply of M×K and K×N matrix values; used in linear algebra kernels and to lower to hardware MMA/GEMM instructions.

name: llvm.matrix.transpose
description: Transposes a matrix value; used to match operand layout, improve locality, or implement algorithms needing transposed inputs.

name: llvm.maximum
description: IEEE-754 maximum: returns the larger operand, propagates NaNs, +0 preferred over −0; used when strict NaN semantics are required.

name: llvm.maxnum
description: Numeric maximum (like fmax): returns the non-NaN if one is NaN (NaN only if both are NaN), +0 over −0; used in math code tolerant of NaNs.

name: llvm.memcpy
description: Copies a non-overlapping memory block from src to dst; lowered to optimal loops or libcalls; used for struct/array copies.

name: llvm.memcpy.element.unordered.atomic
description: memcpy done via element-sized unordered atomic accesses to prevent tearing; used in concurrent/lock-free code needing atomicity without ordering.

name: llvm.memcpy.inline
description: Inlined memcpy for known small constant sizes (no libcall); used to emit straight-line copy sequences.

name: llvm.memmove
description: Copies a memory block allowing overlap; lowered to memmove/libcall or directional loops; used when src/dst may alias.

name: llvm.memmove.element.unordered.atomic
description: Memmove via element-sized unordered atomic accesses; used for overlapping copies that also require non-tearing atomic transfers.

name: llvm.memset
description: Fills a memory block with a byte value; lowered to memset/libcall or store loops; used for initialization/zeroing.

name: llvm.memset.element.unordered.atomic
description: Memset via element-sized unordered atomic stores to avoid tearing; used to initialize shared data atomically without ordering.

name: llvm.memset.inline
description: Inlined memset for known small constant sizes (no libcall); used to generate straight-line store sequences.

name: llvm.minimum
description: IEEE-754 minimum: returns the smaller operand, propagates NaNs, −0 preferred over +0; used when strict NaN semantics are required.

name: llvm.minnum
description: Numeric minimum (like fmin): returns the non-NaN if one is NaN (NaN only if both are NaN), −0 over +0; used in math code tolerant of NaNs.

name: llvm.nearbyint
description: Rounds a floating-point value to an integral value using the current FP rounding mode without raising inexact; used in numeric kernels that must respect rounding modes.

name: llvm.objc.arc.annotation.bottomup.bbend
description: No-op marker for end of a basic block in ARC bottom-up dataflow; used by the ObjC ARC optimizer to bound retain/release motion.

name: llvm.objc.arc.annotation.bottomup.bbstart
description: No-op marker for start of a basic block in ARC bottom-up dataflow; used by the ObjC ARC optimizer to bound retain/release motion.

name: llvm.objc.arc.annotation.topdown.bbend
description: No-op marker for end of a basic block in ARC top-down dataflow; used by the ObjC ARC optimizer to bound retain/release motion.

name: llvm.objc.arc.annotation.topdown.bbstart
description: No-op marker for start of a basic block in ARC top-down dataflow; used by the ObjC ARC optimizer to bound retain/release motion.

name: llvm.objc.autorelease
description: Adds an object to the current autorelease pool and returns it; used to hand out +0 objects or balance retains under ARC/MRR.

name: llvm.objc.autoreleasePoolPop
description: Pops (drains) an autorelease pool created by Push given its token; used to end @autoreleasepool scopes and control temporary object lifetimes.

name: llvm.objc.autoreleasePoolPush
description: Pushes a new autorelease pool and returns a pool token; used to start @autoreleasepool scopes, often around tight loops.

name: llvm.objc.autoreleaseReturnValue
description: Specialized autorelease for function return values enabling the caller’s retainAutoreleasedReturnValue fast path; used to optimize object-returning functions under ARC.

name: llvm.objc.clang.arc.noop.use
description: Front-end no-op use to keep a value “alive” for optimization/lifetime purposes without ARC semantics; used to prevent DCE without affecting ARC analysis.

name: llvm.objc.clang.arc.use
description: ARC-visible use that extends the lifetime of an object and constrains refcount motion; used around bridging/consumed uses to inform ARC optimizer.

name: llvm.objc.copyWeak
description: Copies a __weak reference to another weak slot with proper registration; used when assigning between weak variables.

name: llvm.objc.destroyWeak
description: Clears and deregisters a __weak reference slot; used at end of a weak variable’s lifetime or before overwriting it.

name: llvm.objc.initWeak
description: Initializes a __weak reference slot with an object, registering it for zeroing; used when creating weak variables.

name: llvm.objc.loadWeak
description: Loads the current value of a __weak reference at +0 (unretained); used for ephemeral uses where the value isn’t kept beyond the immediate operation.

name: llvm.objc.loadWeakRetained
description: Atomically loads a __weak reference and retains it (+1); used when promoting a weak value to a strong one or when it must stay alive.

name: llvm.objc.moveWeak
description: Moves a __weak reference from one slot to another, clearing the source; used in assignments/moves where the source should be nulled.

name: llvm.objc.release
description: Decrements an object’s retain count (release message); used to balance retains and manage +1 ownership under ARC/MRR.

name: llvm.objc.retain
description: Increments an object’s retain count and returns it; used to acquire +1 ownership or extend lifetimes.

name: llvm.objc.retain.autorelease
description: Retains then autoreleases an object and returns it (+0 overall); used to hand out autoreleased values or convert +1 to +0.

name: llvm.objc.retainAutorelease
description: Retain then autorelease an Objective‑C object in one step; used under ARC/MRR around call boundaries to balance temporary ownership with fewer runtime calls.

name: llvm.objc.retainAutoreleaseReturnValue
description: Retain+autorelease a function’s return object in the callee; participates in the optimized return‑value handoff with callers to reduce retain/release traffic.

name: llvm.objc.retainAutoreleasedReturnValue
description: Specialized retain of a just‑returned autoreleased object in the caller; claims the optimized return value to avoid extra retains/releases.

name: llvm.objc.retainBlock
description: Retain (and copy to heap if needed) an Objective‑C block; used when storing/passing blocks beyond their stack lifetime.

name: llvm.objc.retainedObject
description: Convert a +1 retained void*/CF pointer into an ObjC object with ownership transfer; used to implement __bridge_transfer and CFCreate→ARC handoff.

name: llvm.objc.storeStrong
description: Store to a __strong reference with correct retain/release ordering and barriers; used for strong variable/property assignments (lowers to objc_storeStrong).

name: llvm.objc.storeWeak
description: Store to a __weak reference with zeroing‑weak semantics; used for weak variable/property assignments (lowers to objc_storeWeak).

name: llvm.objc.sync.enter
description: Enter an @synchronized block by acquiring the ObjC monitor lock on an object; ensures mutual exclusion.

name: llvm.objc.sync.exit
description: Exit an @synchronized block by releasing the ObjC monitor lock; used on normal and exceptional exits.

name: llvm.objc.unretainedObject
description: Convert a void*/CF pointer to an ObjC object without changing ownership; used to implement __bridge (no transfer).

name: llvm.objc.unretainedPointer
description: Convert an ObjC object to a void*/CF pointer without changing ownership; used for __bridge casts.

name: llvm.objc.unsafeClaimAutoreleasedReturnValue
description: Claim an autoreleased return value without an extra retain via runtime handoff; used immediately after calls returning autoreleased objects to elide retain/release.

name: llvm.objectsize
description: Estimate the size of the object a pointer can refer to (upper/lower bound); used by __builtin_object_size, fortify checks, and size‑based optimizations.

name: llvm.pcmarker
description: Insert a target‑specific program‑counter marker for performance tools; used to annotate code regions for profilers/sampling and tracing.

name: llvm.pow
description: Floating‑point power x^y; used in numerical code, lowered to libm or expanded with fast‑math optimizations.

name: llvm.powi
description: Floating‑point power with integer exponent x^n; enables faster expansions (e.g., repeated multiplies) and constant folding in numeric kernels.

name: llvm.prefetch
description: Hint to prefetch a memory address (read/write, locality, cache level); used to hide memory latency in hot loops and streaming workloads.

name: llvm.preserve.array.access.index
description: Preserve the high‑level array subscript for a pointer computation; used by __builtin_preserve_access_index (e.g., BPF CO‑RE, hardening) so later tools can reconstruct element access.

name: llvm.preserve.static.offset
description: Preserve a known static byte offset from a base object; used with __builtin_preserve_access_index to recover exact offsets after optimization.

name: llvm.preserve.struct.access.index
description: Preserve the high‑level struct field index path for a pointer computation; used by __builtin_preserve_access_index (notably BPF CO‑RE) to enable field‑offset relocation.

name: llvm.preserve.union.access.index
description: Non-semantic marker recording which union member is accessed; preserves alias/type info for optimizations and sanitizers across IR transformations.

name: llvm.pseudoprobe
description: Inserts a pseudo profiling probe (no direct code until lowering); used for sample-based PGO/AutoFDO and code layout with inline-context awareness.

name: llvm.ptr.annotation
description: Attaches a source-level annotation to a pointer and returns it; carries __attribute__((annotate)) strings for tools/analyses and optimization hints.

name: llvm.ptrauth.auth
description: Authenticates a pointer with a key/discriminator and returns the authenticated (stripped) pointer; used before dereferencing/calling PAC-signed pointers on AArch64.

name: llvm.ptrauth.blend
description: Blends a small integer (e.g., address) with a 32-bit value to derive a diversified discriminator; used to vary pointer-auth signatures per use-site.

name: llvm.ptrauth.resign
description: Re-signs a pointer by authenticating with old key/discriminator then signing with a new pair; used when casting or moving pointers across protection domains.

name: llvm.ptrauth.sign
description: Signs a pointer with a key and discriminator to add a PAC; used when storing/transporting pointers to protect against corruption.

name: llvm.ptrauth.sign.generic
description: Signs a non-pointer 64-bit value with a key and discriminator; used to authenticate data or create PAC-like tags for non-pointer payloads.

name: llvm.ptrauth.strip
description: Strips the authentication code from a pointer without verification; used for logging/comparison or when authentication isn’t required.

name: llvm.ptrmask
description: Applies a mask to a pointer (ptr & mask); used to clear tag bits (e.g., AArch64 TBI/MTE) or enforce alignment by zeroing low bits.

name: llvm.public.type.test
description: Type-based CFI/WPD test against a public type-id set; used to guard indirect calls/vtable uses and enable jump-table lowering across DSOs/LTO.

name: llvm.read_register
description: Reads an architectural register named by a constant string; may be optimized/reordered; used to access special regs (e.g., thread pointer).

name: llvm.read_volatile_register
description: Volatile read of an architectural register; not eliminated or reordered; used for device/system registers where every read must occur.

name: llvm.readcyclecounter
description: Reads a high-resolution cycle counter (may be frequency-dependent); used for low-level timing and profiling.

name: llvm.readsteadycounter
description: Reads a monotonic, frequency-invariant counter if available; used for elapsed-time measurements unaffected by CPU frequency scaling.

name: llvm.reset.fpenv
description: Resets the floating-point environment/exception state to default and clears accrued flags; used around regions with FENV access or strict FP.

name: llvm.reset.fpmode
description: Resets the current floating-point mode (e.g., rounding, exception behavior) to default; used when leaving scoped non-default FP modes.

name: llvm.returnaddress
description: Returns the return address of the current or a caller frame; used for backtraces, logging, and sanitizer instrumentation (nonportable, limited).

name: llvm.rint
description: Rounds to an integral value using the current FP rounding mode, raising inexact as appropriate; implements C rint().

name: llvm.round
description: Rounds to nearest integer with halfway cases away from zero; implements C round(), returning a floating-point integral value.

name: llvm.roundeven
description: Rounds a floating-point value to the nearest integer with ties to even; useful when a fixed, deterministic IEEE “banker’s rounding” is required independent of the current FP mode (e.g., financial/math kernels, reproducible numerics).

name: llvm.sadd.sat
description: Signed saturating addition; on overflow clamps to INT_MIN/INT_MAX instead of wrapping; common in DSP, image/audio processing, and fixed-point arithmetic.

name: llvm.sadd.with.overflow
description: Signed add that returns {sum, overflow-flag}; used for checked arithmetic, bounds checking, big-integer libraries, and sanitizers.

name: llvm.scmp
description: Signed three-way compare that yields -1, 0, or 1; used to implement total ordering (e.g., C++20 <=>) efficiently and without overflow pitfalls.

name: llvm.sdiv.fix
description: Signed fixed-point division with a built-in scale (fractional bits) immediate; used in embedded/DSP code to model fixed-point divide precisely.

name: llvm.sdiv.fix.sat
description: Signed fixed-point division with scaling that saturates on overflow; used where fixed-point divide must not wrap (DSP, control systems).

name: llvm.seh.scope.begin
description: Marks the start of a Windows SEH scope; a codegen marker used by MSVC-style __try/__except/__finally lowering for correct unwind tables.

name: llvm.seh.scope.end
description: Marks the end of a Windows SEH scope; pairs with seh.scope.begin to delineate SEH regions for Windows exception handling metadata.

name: llvm.seh.try.begin
description: Marks the start of a Windows SEH try region; used to model __try blocks so the backend can emit proper EH/unwind info.

name: llvm.seh.try.end
description: Marks the end of a Windows SEH try region; paired with seh.try.begin for MSVC SEH lowering.

name: llvm.set.fpenv
description: Sets the active floating-point environment (rounding/exceptions/denormals) state token for constrained FP ops; used to implement C fenv.h (fe{set,update}env) and strict FP pragmas.

name: llvm.set.fpmode
description: Sets the current floating-point mode (e.g., exception behavior, denormal handling) guiding constrained FP intrinsics; used by frontends honoring strict/fast FP controls and pragmas.

name: llvm.set.loop.iterations
description: Provides a loop’s trip count to the backend for hardware loop lowering; used on targets like ARM to materialize low-overhead/hardware loops.

name: llvm.set.rounding
description: Sets the dynamic floating-point rounding mode; used to implement fesetround, #pragma STDC FENV_ROUND, and numerics code needing mode changes.

name: llvm.sideeffect
description: Opaque side-effect barrier with no operands; prevents code motion and DCE around it; used to model unknown effects (e.g., inline asm sideeffect), testing, or timing fences.

name: llvm.sin
description: Computes the sine of a floating-point value; maps to libm or hardware instructions; used in signal processing, graphics, and scientific code.

name: llvm.sinh
description: Computes the hyperbolic sine; used in scientific/numerical applications and special functions.

name: llvm.smax
description: Element-wise signed maximum; used for clamp operations, min/max chains, and to leverage ISA smax instructions in vectorized code.

name: llvm.smin
description: Element-wise signed minimum; used for clamp operations, min/max chains, and lowering to ISA smin instructions.

name: llvm.smul.fix
description: Signed fixed-point multiply with a built-in scale (fractional bits) immediate; used in DSP/embedded kernels to model precise fixed-point multiply.

name: llvm.smul.fix.sat
description: Signed fixed-point multiply with scaling and saturation; used in DSP, audio/image processing, and embedded fixed-point math.

name: llvm.smul.with.overflow
description: Signed integer multiply returning {result, overflow flag}; used for checked arithmetic, safe-int libraries, and compiler overflow checks.

name: llvm.sponentry
description: Returns the stack pointer as it was on function entry; used for stack-clash probing, VLAs/alloca deallocation, and stack-usage checks.

name: llvm.sqrt
description: Floating-point square root; uses hardware sqrt or libm; common in numerics, graphics, and scientific code.

name: llvm.ssa.copy
description: No-op value copy preserving SSA identity; used to constrain codegen (e.g., keep copies for register allocation/O0) without changing semantics.

name: llvm.sshl.sat
description: Signed left shift with saturation on overflow; used in fixed-point/DSP code to clamp results instead of invoking UB.

name: llvm.ssub.sat
description: Signed saturating subtraction; clamps to min/max on overflow; used in DSP, image/audio processing, and saturating arithmetic.

name: llvm.ssub.with.overflow
description: Signed subtraction returning {result, overflow flag}; used for checked arithmetic and bounds/index calculations.

name: llvm.stackguard
description: Materializes the platform’s stack-canary guard (value/pointer); used by stack protector prologues.

name: llvm.stackprotector
description: Marks points to set/check a stack canary; lowered to prologue/epilogue code for -fstack-protector.

name: llvm.stackrestore
description: Restores the stack pointer to a token from llvm.stacksave; used to free dynamic stack allocations/temps.

name: llvm.stacksave
description: Captures the current stack pointer as an opaque token; paired with llvm.stackrestore around dynamic allocas.

name: llvm.start.loop.iterations
description: Initialize hardware loop trip count and indicate if nonzero; used to form low-overhead hardware loops (e.g., ARM/Hexagon).

name: llvm.strip.invariant.group
description: Removes invariant.group from a pointer, returning the underlying pointer; used before pointer escapes/casts to avoid over-optimistic aliasing.

name: llvm.swift.async.context.addr
description: Returns the address of the current Swift async context; used by the Swift compiler/runtime to implement async/await.

name: llvm.tan
description: Floating-point tangent; lowered to hardware or libm; used in trigonometric computations.

name: llvm.tanh
description: Floating-point hyperbolic tangent; used in numerics and ML activation functions.

name: llvm.test.set.loop.iterations
description: Pseudo to set and test hardware loop iterations in one step; used by backends when forming low-overhead loops.

name: llvm.test.start.loop.iterations
description: Pseudo to start and test a hardware loop counter; used during codegen/legalization of low-overhead loops.

name: llvm.thread.pointer
description: Returns the current thread-pointer register value; used for fast TLS access and language runtime internals.

name: llvm.threadlocal.address
description: Computes the per‑thread address of a thread‑local object; used to access C/C++ thread_local/__thread variables on targets with special TLS models.

name: llvm.trap
description: Causes an immediate trap/abort; used for unreachable code, assert/abort paths, and sanitizer fast‑fail.

name: llvm.trunc
description: Floating‑point truncate toward zero (remove fractional part); used to implement C/C++/libm trunc and fast rounding.

name: llvm.type.checked.load
description: Loads a pointer and checks it against type metadata, returning (ptr, success); used by CFI and whole‑program devirtualization of virtual calls.

name: llvm.type.checked.load.relative
description: Like type.checked.load but for relative (e.g., PC‑relative) pointer encodings; used with relative vtables and CFI/devirt.

name: llvm.type.test
description: Tests whether a pointer refers to an object with given type metadata; used for CFI, vcall guards, and WPD devirtualization.

name: llvm.uadd.sat
description: Unsigned saturating add (clamps at max on overflow); used in image/DSP code and SIMD‑friendly arithmetic.

name: llvm.uadd.with.overflow
description: Unsigned add returning both sum and overflow flag; used for checked math, big integers, and bounds calculations.

name: llvm.ubsantrap
description: UBSan trap with a code; used to terminate immediately on undefined behavior with minimal runtime.

name: llvm.ucmp
description: Unsigned integer comparison producing a boolean result; typically maps to icmp ult/ugt/etc. and used by some builtins/combines.

name: llvm.udiv.fix
description: Unsigned fixed‑point division with a specified fractional scale; used for C fixed‑point types (_Accum/_Fract) and Q‑format math.

name: llvm.udiv.fix.sat
description: Unsigned fixed‑point division with saturation on overflow; used when fixed‑point division must clamp results.

name: llvm.umax
description: Unsigned maximum of two integers; used for bounds clamping and branchless min/max.

name: llvm.umin
description: Unsigned minimum of two integers; used for bounds clamping and branchless min/max.

name: llvm.umul.fix
description: Unsigned fixed‑point multiply with a specified fractional scale; used for _Accum/_Fract arithmetic and Q‑format DSP.

name: llvm.umul.fix.sat
description: Unsigned fixed‑point multiply with saturation on overflow; used in robust fixed‑point DSP/image pipelines.

name: llvm.umul.with.overflow
description: Unsigned multiply returning product and overflow flag; used for checked math, big integers, and capacity checks.

name: llvm.ushl.sat
description: Unsigned saturating left shift (clamps instead of overflowing); used for fixed‑point scaling and packing.

name: llvm.usub.sat
description: Unsigned saturating subtract (floors at 0); used in image/DSP code and clamping arithmetic.

name: llvm.usub.with.overflow
description: Unsigned subtract returning difference and overflow/borrow flag; used for checked arithmetic and range validation.

name: llvm.va_copy
description: Copy an existing va_list; used to duplicate iteration state over C/C++ variadic arguments when you need to traverse args multiple times.

name: llvm.va_end
description: Finalize/cleanup a va_list; used at the end of processing variadic arguments to satisfy ABI requirements.

name: llvm.va_start
description: Initialize a va_list for a variadic function; used to begin accessing ... arguments in C/C++.

name: llvm.var.annotation
description: Attach a string annotation to a local (stack) variable; used by frontends for __attribute__((annotate)), and by tools/analyses/sanitizers to tag variables.

name: llvm.vector.deinterleave2
description: Split an interleaved vector [a0,b0,a1,b1,…] into two vectors of evens/odds; used to undo AoS loads, match NEON unzip/ARM SVE unzips/x86 unpack patterns in vectorized loops.

name: llvm.vector.extract
description: Extract a contiguous subvector starting at a given lane index; used to take low/high halves or sliding windows, mapping to SVE ext/slide, AVX vextract, and shuffle sequences.

name: llvm.vector.insert
description: Insert a subvector into a destination vector at a given lane index; used to build/concatenate vectors or splice pieces, mapping to AVX vinsert, SVE splice, and shuffles.

name: llvm.vector.interleave2
description: Interleave two vectors element-wise into one [a0,b0,a1,b1,…]; used to prepare interleaved stores/loads, AoS formation, and to match zip/pack instructions.

name: llvm.vector.reduce.add
description: Horizontal sum of integer lanes to a scalar; used for reductions in vectorized loops (e.g., sums, dot products).

name: llvm.vector.reduce.and
description: Bitwise AND across lanes to a scalar; used to check “all-true” masks or combine per-lane flags.

name: llvm.vector.reduce.fadd
description: Horizontal sum of floating-point lanes to a scalar; used for numeric reductions in vectorized code (honors FP semantics/fast-math as applicable).

name: llvm.vector.reduce.fmax
description: Floating-point maximum across lanes with “num” semantics (choose the numeric operand if one is NaN); used for max reductions that ignore NaNs, matching libm fmax behavior.

name: llvm.vector.reduce.fmaximum
description: Floating-point maximum across lanes with NaN-propagating semantics (result is NaN if any lane is NaN); used when strict IEEE-754 maximum is required.

name: llvm.vector.reduce.fmin
description: Floating-point minimum across lanes with “num” semantics (choose the numeric operand if one is NaN); used for min reductions that ignore NaNs, matching libm fmin.

name: llvm.vector.reduce.fminimum
description: Floating-point minimum across lanes with NaN-propagating semantics; used for strict IEEE-754 minimum reductions.

name: llvm.vector.reduce.fmul
description: Horizontal product of floating-point lanes to a scalar; used in specialized numeric kernels needing multiplicative reductions.

name: llvm.vector.reduce.mul
description: Horizontal product of integer lanes to a scalar; used in specialized math, hashing, or probabilistic computations.

name: llvm.vector.reduce.or
description: Bitwise OR across lanes to a scalar; used to test “any-true” conditions or accumulate feature flags.

name: llvm.vector.reduce.smax
description: Signed integer maximum across lanes to a scalar; used to find the largest signed value in vectorized scans.

name: llvm.vector.reduce.smin
description: Signed integer minimum across lanes to a scalar; used to find the smallest signed value in vectorized scans.

name: llvm.vector.reduce.umax
description: Reduces a vector to its unsigned maximum element; useful for min/max scans in SIMD loops (e.g., clamps, image/video).

name: llvm.vector.reduce.umin
description: Reduces a vector to its unsigned minimum element; useful for min/max scans, histograms, and thresholding.

name: llvm.vector.reduce.xor
description: XOR-reduces all elements to a single value; useful for parity checks, simple checksums, and hash folding.

name: llvm.vector.reverse
description: Reverses lane order in a vector; useful for sequence reversal, permutation stages, and bitonic/sort kernels.

name: llvm.vector.splice
description: Forms a vector by extracting a contiguous slice from the logical concatenation of two vectors at an immediate index; useful for sliding-window/shift-with-insert in SVE/RVV, stencils, and FIR filters.

name: llvm.vp.abs
description: Predicated per-lane integer absolute value with mask/EVL; useful in VLA SIMD loops for |x| under masks and tail handling.

name: llvm.vp.add
description: Predicated per-lane integer addition with mask/EVL; useful for masked vector loops, sparse updates, and tail-aware accumulations.

name: llvm.vp.and
description: Predicated per-lane bitwise AND with mask/EVL; useful for masked bit filtering and flag manipulation.

name: llvm.vp.ashr
description: Predicated per-lane arithmetic right shift with mask/EVL; useful in fixed-point math and sign-preserving scaling.

name: llvm.vp.bitreverse
description: Predicated per-lane bit-order reversal with mask/EVL; useful in FFT bit-reversal stages and cryptographic transforms.

name: llvm.vp.bswap
description: Predicated per-lane byte swap with mask/EVL; useful for endianness conversion and parsing/serialization.

name: llvm.vp.ceil
description: Predicated per-lane floating-point ceil with mask/EVL; useful in math kernels, quantization, and discretization.

name: llvm.vp.copysign
description: Predicated per-lane copy sign (magnitude from op0, sign from op1) with mask/EVL; useful for enforcing sign conventions and odd/even function composition.

name: llvm.vp.ctlz
description: Predicated per-lane count leading zeros with mask/EVL; useful for normalization, log2/priority encoders, and leading-one detection.

name: llvm.vp.ctpop
description: Predicated per-lane population count with mask/EVL; useful in Hamming weight, bitset algorithms, and cryptography.

name: llvm.vp.cttz
description: Predicated per-lane count trailing zeros with mask/EVL; useful for isolating low set bits, alignment, and priority encoders.

name: llvm.vp.cttz.elts
description: Counts trailing zero elements (consecutive lanes equal to zero) in a vector, under mask/EVL; useful to find first active lane or effective length in predicate/mask vectors.

name: llvm.vp.fabs
description: Predicated per-lane floating-point absolute value with mask/EVL; useful in norms, error metrics, and math kernels.

name: llvm.vp.fadd
description: Predicated per-lane floating-point addition with mask/EVL; useful for masked FP updates and tail-aware accumulations.

name: llvm.vp.fcmp
description: Predicated per-lane floating-point compare with mask/EVL; returns an i1 mask for conditional updates, masking, and reductions.

name: llvm.vp.fdiv
description: Vector-predicated floating-point divide (mask + EVL); used for masked/tail SIMD divisions without touching inactive lanes.

name: llvm.vp.floor
description: Vector-predicated floor per lane; useful in math/graphics kernels where only some lanes are active.

name: llvm.vp.fma
description: Vector-predicated fused multiply-add (a*b + c in one rounding); ideal for masked dot products and MAC-heavy loops.

name: llvm.vp.fmul
description: Vector-predicated floating-point multiply; used in masked vectorized arithmetic and tail handling.

name: llvm.vp.fmuladd
description: Vector-predicated non-fused mul+add (a*b + c as two ops); use when contraction to FMA is disallowed in masked loops.

name: llvm.vp.fneg
description: Vector-predicated floating negate (-x); sign flips in masked computations and tails.

name: llvm.vp.fpext
description: Vector-predicated FP extend (e.g., f32→f64); widen precision for active lanes only in vectorized code.

name: llvm.vp.fptosi
description: Vector-predicated FP-to-signed-int conversion; produce integer indices/results from FP under a mask.

name: llvm.vp.fptoui
description: Vector-predicated FP-to-unsigned-int conversion; common in graphics/image and quantization under masks.

name: llvm.vp.fptrunc
description: Vector-predicated FP truncate (e.g., f64→f32); narrow results for active lanes to save bandwidth.

name: llvm.vp.frem
description: Vector-predicated floating remainder per lane; used for periodic wrap/arithmetic on reals in masked loops.

name: llvm.vp.fshl
description: Vector-predicated funnel shift left (rotate/merge bits from two ints); used in crypto, hashing, and bit tricks under masks.

name: llvm.vp.fshr
description: Vector-predicated funnel shift right; enables rotations/bitfield extraction with predication.

name: llvm.vp.fsub
description: Vector-predicated floating-point subtraction; used in masked SIMD math and tail processing.

name: llvm.vp.gather
description: Vector-predicated gather load from scattered addresses; only active lanes are accessed, enabling safe indirect SIMD loads.

name: llvm.vp.icmp
description: Vector-predicated integer compare; computes per-lane boolean results for active lanes, forming masks for further predicated ops.

name: llvm.vp.inttoptr
description: Vector-predicated int-to-pointer cast; forms vector pointers/addresses for active lanes in vectorized memory operations.

name: llvm.vp.is.fpclass
description: Vector-predicated FP classification (NaN, Inf, subnormal, etc.); identify special values per lane under a mask.

name: llvm.vp.llrint
description: Vector-predicated llrint: rounds FP to signed 64-bit by current rounding mode; for strict numeric conversions under masks.

name: llvm.vp.load
description: Vector-predicated load with mask + EVL; reads only active lanes, avoiding faults on inactive lanes for masked/tail loads.

name: llvm.vp.lrint
description: Masked/EVL float-to-signed-integer rounding per current FP rounding mode (like C lrint); used for vectorized, exception-aware conversions.

name: llvm.vp.lshr
description: Masked/EVL logical right shift of integers; used in bitfield unpacking, cryptography, and general bit-manipulation.

name: llvm.vp.maximum
description: Masked/EVL IEEE maximum (NaNs propagate); used when strict IEEE semantics are required in vectorized max operations.

name: llvm.vp.maxnum
description: Masked/EVL numeric maximum (fmax semantics: choose the number if the other is NaN, both NaN→NaN); used for robust max/clamp ignoring NaNs.

name: llvm.vp.merge
description: Masked/EVL lane-wise merge: take from first vector where mask true, else from second (passthrough); used to blend masked results with prior values.

name: llvm.vp.minimum
description: Masked/EVL IEEE minimum (NaNs propagate); used for strict min operations in vector code.

name: llvm.vp.minnum
description: Masked/EVL numeric minimum (fmin semantics: choose the number if the other is NaN, both NaN→NaN); used for min/clamp that ignores NaNs.

name: llvm.vp.mul
description: Masked/EVL integer multiply; used in vectorized arithmetic, polynomial math, and hashing.

name: llvm.vp.nearbyint
description: Masked/EVL round-to-integer-value in FP domain per current rounding mode, without raising inexact; used for vectorized rounding.

name: llvm.vp.or
description: Masked/EVL bitwise OR of integers; used for combining flags and building bitmasks.

name: llvm.vp.ptrtoint
description: Masked/EVL pointer-to-integer cast per lane; used in vectorized address arithmetic and pointer hashing.

name: llvm.vp.reduce.add
description: Masked/EVL horizontal sum of integer lanes (with an accumulator); used for vectorized reductions like summing elements.

name: llvm.vp.reduce.and
description: Masked/EVL horizontal bitwise AND reduction; used for “all-set” checks and combining masks.

name: llvm.vp.reduce.fadd
description: Masked/EVL horizontal floating-point sum (with a start value; honors FP flags); used for vectorized sums and dot products.

name: llvm.vp.reduce.fmax
description: Masked/EVL horizontal fmax reduction (numeric max: prefers numbers over NaNs); used to find a max while ignoring NaNs.

name: llvm.vp.reduce.fmaximum
description: Masked/EVL horizontal IEEE maximum reduction (NaNs propagate); used when NaNs must poison the reduction result.

name: llvm.vp.reduce.fmin
description: Masked/EVL horizontal fmin reduction (numeric min: prefers numbers over NaNs); used to find a min while ignoring NaNs.

name: llvm.vp.reduce.fminimum
description: Masked/EVL horizontal IEEE minimum reduction (NaNs propagate); used for strict IEEE-compliant min reductions.

name: llvm.vp.reduce.fmul
description: Masked/EVL horizontal floating-point product (with a start value); used for product reductions, likelihoods, and geometric means.

name: llvm.vp.reduce.mul
description: Masked/EVL horizontal integer product; used in polynomial evaluations and hash/product accumulations.

name: llvm.vp.reduce.or
description: Masked vector reduction computing bitwise OR of active lanes (with EVL); used to aggregate flags/masks in predicated vector loops (e.g., SVE/AVX-512).

name: llvm.vp.reduce.smax
description: Masked reduction to the signed maximum over active lanes; used for max scans over arrays with tail predication.

name: llvm.vp.reduce.smin
description: Masked reduction to the signed minimum over active lanes; used for min scans in vectorized loops with masked tails.

name: llvm.vp.reduce.umax
description: Masked reduction to the unsigned maximum over active lanes; used when data are unsigned (e.g., bytes) in parallel scans.

name: llvm.vp.reduce.umin
description: Masked reduction to the unsigned minimum over active lanes; used for range minima on unsigned data.

name: llvm.vp.reduce.xor
description: Masked reduction computing XOR of active lanes; used for parity bits, simple checksums, and hashing.

name: llvm.vp.rint
description: Per-lane round-to-integer using current FP rounding mode (returns FP), with mask/EVL; used in numerics that must honor the FP environment under predication.

name: llvm.vp.round
description: Per-lane round to nearest, ties away from zero (returns FP), with mask/EVL; used to implement C round() in vectorized code.

name: llvm.vp.roundeven
description: Per-lane round to nearest, ties to even (banker’s rounding), masked/EVL; used for IEEE-conformant rounding in SIMD code.

name: llvm.vp.roundtozero
description: Per-lane round toward zero (truncate fractional part, returns FP), masked/EVL; used for fast truncation when vectorizing conversions.

name: llvm.vp.sadd.sat
description: Per-lane signed saturating add under mask/EVL; clamps on overflow; used in DSP, image/audio processing, and robust SIMD arithmetic.

name: llvm.vp.scatter
description: Masked vector scatter store to non-contiguous addresses with EVL; used for irregular memory writes in vectorized loops (e.g., sparse/indirect stores).

name: llvm.vp.sdiv
description: Per-lane signed integer division with mask/EVL; used when vectorizing divides with predication/tail handling.

name: llvm.vp.select
description: Masked per-lane select/blend between two vectors with EVL; used for if-conversion and blending results in vector code.

name: llvm.vp.sext
description: Masked sign-extension from narrower to wider integer element type with EVL; used in widening operations and signed conversions.

name: llvm.vp.shl
description: Per-lane left shift with mask/EVL; used in bitfield operations and scaling by powers of two in vectorized code.

name: llvm.vp.sitofp
description: Per-lane signed int to float conversion under mask/EVL; used for vectorized casts and mixed-precision math.

name: llvm.vp.smax
description: Per-lane signed maximum between two vectors with mask/EVL; used for clamping, ReLU-like ops, and elementwise maxima.

name: llvm.vp.smin
description: Per-lane signed minimum between two vectors with mask/EVL; used for clamping and elementwise minima.

name: llvm.vp.sqrt
description: Per-lane square root with mask/EVL; used in math kernels (norms, distance, normalization) with predication/tail folding.

name: llvm.vp.srem
description: Predicated per-lane signed remainder (mask + EVL control); used for masked/tail vectorized modulo on signed integers.

name: llvm.vp.ssub.sat
description: Predicated signed saturating subtract (clamps on overflow); used in DSP, image/audio pipelines, and clamped differences.

name: llvm.vp.store
description: Predicated vector store to memory (only active lanes written, EVL limits lanes); used for masked/tail stores in vectorized loops.

name: llvm.vp.sub
description: Predicated per-lane integer subtraction with wrap semantics; used in masked arithmetic and loop predication.

name: llvm.vp.trunc
description: Predicated per-lane integer narrowing (truncate high bits); used in vectorized narrowing conversions and packing.

name: llvm.vp.uadd.sat
description: Predicated unsigned saturating add (clamps at max); used in pixel ops, accumulators, video/audio processing.

name: llvm.vp.udiv
description: Predicated per-lane unsigned division (div-by-zero is UB per lane); used for masked/tail vector divides.

name: llvm.vp.uitofp
description: Predicated per-lane unsigned integer to float conversion; used in vectorized numeric conversions.

name: llvm.vp.umax
description: Predicated per-lane unsigned maximum; used for clamps, min/max filters, and reductions.

name: llvm.vp.umin
description: Predicated per-lane unsigned minimum; used for clamps, min/max filters, and reductions.

name: llvm.vp.urem
description: Predicated per-lane unsigned remainder (div-by-zero is UB per lane); used for masked modulo operations.

name: llvm.vp.usub.sat
description: Predicated unsigned saturating subtract (floors at 0); used for underflow-safe diffs, image/video ops.

name: llvm.vp.xor
description: Predicated per-lane bitwise XOR; used for masked bit ops, parity, hashing, and toggling flags.

name: llvm.vp.zext
description: Predicated per-lane zero-extension to wider integer; used in vectorized widening and unsigned promotion.

name: llvm.vscale
description: Returns the runtime scale factor for scalable vectors (vscale); used to size loops/arrays and compute trip counts for SVE/RVV.

name: llvm.write_register
description: Writes a value to a target-specific hardware register by name; used in low-level runtimes, kernels, and embedded code for CSR/special-reg control.

name: llvm.xray.customevent
description: Emits a user-defined payload event into the XRay trace at runtime; used to annotate traces with app-specific markers.

name: llvm.xray.typedevent
description: Emits a typed XRay event (type ID + payload) into the trace; used for structured, domain-specific runtime annotations.
